options.name: -p
options.description: Port to connect to on the remote host

options.name: -i
options.description: Select a file from which the identity (private key) is read

options.name: -l
options.description: Specify the user to log in as on the remote machine

options.name: -L
options.description: Forward connections from a local port to a remote address

options.name: -R
options.description: Forward connections from a remote port to a local address

options.name: -D
options.description: Specify a local dynamic application-level port forwarding (SOCKS proxy)

options.name: -N
options.description: Do not execute a remote command (useful for port forwarding only)

options.name: -f
options.description: Go to background just before command execution

options.name: -v
options.description: Verbose mode, print debugging messages

options.name: -o
options.description: Specify an option in the format used in the configuration file

options.name: -A
options.description: Enable forwarding of the authentication agent connection

options.name: -C
options.description: Request compression of all data

options.name: -X
options.description: Enable X11 forwarding

options.name: -Y
options.description: Enable trusted X11 forwarding

options.name: -t
options.description: Force pseudo-terminal allocation

options.name: -T
options.description: Disable pseudo-terminal allocation

options.name: -q
options.description: Quiet mode, suppress most warning and diagnostic messages

options.name: -J
options.description: Connect to the target host via a jump host (ProxyJump)

options.name: -W
options.description: Forward standard input and output to a host and port via the secure channel

options.name: -F
options.description: Specify an alternative per-user configuration file
Force use of IPv4 addresses only
Force use of IPv6 addresses only
